header.description: Starts the workflow when Airtable events occur

pollTimes.description: Time at which polling should occur

hour.description: The hour of the day to trigger (24h format)

minute.description: The minute of the day to trigger

dayOfMonth.description: The day of the month to trigger

cronExpression.description:
Use custom cron expression. Values and ranges as follows:
  • Seconds: 0-59
  • Minutes: 0 - 59
  • Hours: 0 - 23
  • Day of Month: 1 - 31
  • Months: 0 - 11 (Jan - Dec)
  • Day of Week: 0 - 6 (Sun - Sat)

value.description: All how many X minutes/hours it should trigger

unit.description: If it should trigger all X minutes or hours

hour.description: The hour of the day to trigger (24h format)

minute.description: The minute of the day to trigger

dayOfMonth.description: The day of the month to trigger

cronExpression.description:
Use custom cron expression. Values and ranges as follows:
  • Seconds: 0-59
  • Minutes: 0 - 59
  • Hours: 0 - 23
  • Day of Month: 1 - 31
  • Months: 0 - 11 (Jan - Dec)
  • Day of Week: 0 - 6 (Sun - Sat)

value.description: All how many X minutes/hours it should trigger

unit.description: If it should trigger all X minutes or hours

baseId.description: The Airtable Base in which to operate on

triggerField.description: A Created Time or Last Modified Time field that will be used to sort records. If you do not have a Created Time or Last Modified Time field in your schema, please create one, because without this field trigger will not work correctly.

downloadAttachments.description: Whether the attachment fields define in 'Download Fields' will be downloaded

downloadFieldNames.description: Name of the fields of type 'attachment' that should be downloaded. Multiple ones can be defined separated by comma. Case sensitive.

fields.description: Fields to be included in the response. Multiple ones can be set separated by comma. Example: name, id. By default just the trigger field will be included.

formula.description: Formulas may involve functions, numeric operations, logical operations, and text operations that operate on fields. More info here.

viewId.description: The name or ID of a view in the table. If set, only the records in that view will be returned.
